title: Success Story: Overcoming RFE! Our Firm Helped an Iraqi Senior Manager of Data Science Secure NIW Approval via Premium Processing
content:
Client’s Testimonial:
“I’m incredibly grateful to NAILG for guiding me through every step of the NIW petition process, especially when responding to the RFE. It meant a lot to have a team that understood both the technical depth and national importance of my work. Their attention to detail, responsiveness, and strategic framing of my case played a key role in the approval. From initial consultation to premium processing submission, they handled everything with professionalism and care. I highly recommend NAILG to any researcher or professional pursuing the NIW path.”
On June 13 th , 2025, we received another EB-2 NIW ( National Interest Waiver ) approval for a Senior Manager of Data Science in the Field of Artificial Intelligence ( Approval Notice ).
General Field: Artificial Intelligence
Position at the Time of Case Filing : Senior Manager of Data Science
Country of Origin: Iraq
State of Residence at the Time of Filing: North Carolina
Approval Notice Date: June 13 th , 2025
Processing Time: 5 months, 24 days (Premium Processing Requested)
We are pleased to celebrate the approval of an I-140 NIW ( National Interest Waiver ) petition for a distinguished researcher in artificial intelligence. This case, approved following a Request for Evidence (RFE), underscores the national significance of his work in applying advanced AI techniques to real-world challenges in e-commerce and cybersecurity.
With a Ph.D. in computer science and engineering, the petitioner’s research focuses on developing sophisticated machine learning and deep learning frameworks to better predict user intent during online interactions. His goal is to create more secure, efficient, and tailored customer experiences, helping U.S. businesses not only safeguard sensitive systems but also drive user engagement and revenue growth through personalization.
Currently employed in industry, the petitioner leads a data science team tasked with designing cutting-edge AI models that optimize real-time recommendations and strengthen threat detection systems. His work directly contributes to improving decision-making speed, cybersecurity defense, and consumer satisfaction—key pillars of competitiveness in both retail and national security sectors.
To support his petition under the EB-2 NIW category, the following evidence was presented:
● 6 peer-reviewed conference papers, including 3 first-authored,
● 60 citations to date, demonstrating the influence of his research across academic and applied AI fields,
● Peer-reviewed articles published in top-ranked venues in computer science, including one paper that ranks among the top 10% most-cited in its publication year,
● A record of highly novel, influential work on email intent detection, insider threat modeling, and personalized information retrieval.
The petition also included two letters of recommendation from established experts in the AI domain, one of which was from an independent source. These letters provided insight into the originality and utility of his work.
“Moreover, [Client’s] research is furthering deep learning research in cybersecurity and indirectly contributing to the progress of more robust artificial intelligence systems for anomaly detection in various domains, such as fraud detection, healthcare monitoring, and predictive maintenance. Thus, it is imperative that the U.S. support his ongoing work in the field of artificial intelligence.”
--------------------------------------------
North America Immigration Law Group (Chen Immigration Law Associates) is a U.S. immigration law firm dedicated to representing corporations, research institutions, and individuals from all 50 U.S. states regarding I-140 immigration petitions. We specialize in employment-based immigration petition and have a proven record of high success rate for the categories of: EB2- NIW ( National Interest Waiver ), EB1-A ( Alien of Extraordinary Ability ), EB1-B (Outstanding Researcher/Professor) and O-1 ( Alien of Extraordinary Ability ).
Our Sixty-four Thousand I-140 Approvals Provide Unprecedented Insight into the USCIS Adjudication Trend
With nearly 64,000 EB-1A , EB-1B , EB-2 NIW and O-1 cases approved, we have first hand information on the manner in which the USCIS adjudicate I-140 cases. As the USCIS has constantly changed its adjudication standards for the EB-1A , EB-1B and EB-2 NIW categories, our firm's huge database of successful cases gives you unprecedented insight to USCIS adjudication trends. We carefully analyze the data for all of our cases and apply the results of our analyses toward giving our clients up-to-date advice and adapting our strategies such that we remain on par with the ever-shifting landscape of immigration law in the U.S. With us, you will always have access to important updates, strategies, and information so that you can make the most informed decisions about your case.
We Have Helped Tens of Thousands of Clients with Credentials and Backgrounds Similar to Yours
With our exceedingly large number of successful petitions, no matter what credentials you have, no matter your background and field of expertise, no matter your visa status or nationality, chances are we have helped hundreds or even thousands of clients just like you. Our clients are usually impressed with how well we understand their research and work. Our insight and understanding stems from the fact that we have handled many cases with elements similar to yours already, and this helps us devise the best strategies for each individual petition.
Vast Majority of Clients Came to Us Because of Referrals
For years, our firm has attracted new clients based solely on word of mouth, recommendations, and the positive collaboration experiences shared with them by their friends and family. We take pride in our reputation and work hard to ensure that we provide a green card application experience that our clients are happy to share with their friends and colleagues. That is how our cumulative total of approved cases grew from 600 in 2013 to nearly 64,000 in 2025.
Track Record of Success EB-1 and NIW Approvals
